content: Phase I trial design uses dose-escalation methodology: starting at a conservative dose (typically 1/10th of the no-observed-adverse-effect level from animal studies), then incrementally increasing doses in cohorts of 3-6 subjects. Common designs include 3+3 escalation, accelerated titration, or model-based approaches (CRM—continual reassessment method). Subjects are monitored for adverse events, vital signs, ECG changes, and laboratory abnormalities. Pharmacokinetic sampling determines Cmax, Tmax, AUC, half-life, and accumulation. Food-effect and drug-drug interaction sub-studies may be included.
